title: WordPress REST Base Controller Lifecycle

class_overview.name: WP_REST_Controller
class_overview.role: Core base controller for managing and interacting with REST API items. All CRUD stubs return WP_Error('invalid-method', 405) — subclasses must override.

activity_phases.behavior: STUB — calls _doing_it_wrong() if not overridden. Subclass must call register_rest_route() here.
activity_phases.output: Routes registered with WP REST server

activity_phases.behavior: All stubs return WP_Error(status=405). Subclass overrides check current_user_can() or custom auth logic. REST server calls permission check BEFORE action method.
activity_phases.branch: If WP_Error returned → REST server rejects request with error response. If true → proceed to action.

activity_phases.behavior: All stubs return WP_Error(status=405). Subclass implements DB query/write logic.
STUB — maps WP_REST_Request fields to DB object structure. Called internally by create_item/update_item in subclasses before DB insert/update.
STUB — maps DB/model object to REST response shape. Must be overridden. Subclass calls add_additional_fields_to_object() and filter_response_by_context() here.
CONCRETE — calls get_item_schema() then rest_filter_response_by_context($data, $schema, $context). Removes fields whose schema 'context' array does not include the request context (view/edit/embed).
CONCRETE — unwraps WP_REST_Response: calls $response->get_data(), rest_get_server(), WP_REST_Server::get_compact_response_links($response). Injects '_links' key if present.
activity_phases.used_by: get_items() in subclasses iterates items, calls prepare_item_for_response(), then prepare_response_for_collection() per item.

supporting_methods: get_item_schema() → calls add_additional_fields_schema([]); returns JSON Schema array; result cached in $schema
get_public_item_schema() → calls get_item_schema(), strips 'arg_options' from each property
get_collection_params() → returns context, page (default=1, min=1), per_page (default=10, min=1, max=100), search
get_context_param($args=[]) → builds context enum from schema properties; merges with $args
get_fields_for_response($request) → filters schema properties by context + _fields param; always includes _links/_embedded
add_additional_fields_to_object($prepared, $request) → protected; reads $wp_rest_additional_fields global; calls get_callback per registered field
update_additional_fields_for_object($object, $request) → protected; calls update_callback per registered additional field
add_additional_fields_schema($schema) → protected; merges registered field schemas into main schema
decision_points: permission_check returns WP_Error → REST server returns error response (no action called)
permission_check returns true → action method is invoked
filter_response_by_context: field schema has 'context' array → field included only if request context is in that array
get_fields_for_response: _fields param present → intersect with schema fields; absent → return all schema fields for context
prepare_response_for_collection: $response not WP_REST_Response instance → return as-is
add_additional_fields_schema: schema has no 'title' → return schema unchanged
error_type: WP_Error with code 'invalid-method', HTTP status 405 for all unimplemented stubs
important_notes: Abstract class since WP 4.7.0. $schema property (since 5.3.0) caches get_item_schema() result for performance. All CRUD methods + permission checks are template stubs (405 WP_Error). Only prepare_response_for_collection, filter_response_by_context, get_collection_params, get_context_param, get_fields_for_response, sanitize_slug are concrete implementations. The REST server enforces permission-check → action ordering externally.
